title: magnolia acuminata var. candollei
intro: Magnolia acuminata var. candollei, also known as the Sweet Bay Magnolia or Eastern Sweetbay, is a variety of the Magnolia acuminata tree, native to North America and parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Magnolia acuminata var. candollei include relief from digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the leaves, bark, or flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
